mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate declines below 4.5% but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic activity, which raises corporate profits and stock valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and INDPRO rise but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation below 2% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank accommodative policy, which lowers real interest rates and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL remains below 2% but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) above 150,000 signal sustained labor market strength, which increases consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 150,000 but 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.5 million signals strong demand for residential construction, which boosts related industries and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1.5M but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong economic momentum, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS rises and UNRATE falls but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO exceeds 100 but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances.
